ops.title: Editing a *.src.md guide requires re-rendering with the exact cc-guides version CI's checker pins (0.1.2 via action-v1), or the Guides freshness check fails — the header version stamp is part of the byte comparison
ops.body:
Repos scaffolded with a `guides.yml` workflow (cc-notes and siblings) render AGENTS.md/CLAUDE.md from `*.src.md` via cc-guides, and the push-time `check` job byte-compares the rendered artifact INCLUDING the `<!-- cc-guides <version> ... -->` header stamp. `cc-guides` is not on PATH and not on PyPI; the dev checkout `~/Code/cc-guides/bin/cc-guides` stamps its dirty git version (e.g. `cf9f349-dirty`), which the CI checker rejects.

**Why:** `cc-guides check` re-renders with its own binary and compares bytes, so any version mismatch between the rendering binary and CI's pinned checker (`yasyf/cc-guides@action-v1`, currently 0.1.2) reads as STALE.

**How to apply:** after editing a `*.src.md`, download the CI-matching release binary (`gh release download v0.1.2 -R yasyf/cc-guides -p 'cc-guides_darwin_arm64'`), run its `render` then `check` (must print OK), and commit source + artifact together. Infer the pinned version from the header stamp of the currently committed artifacts. Related: [[cc-skills-marketplace-skill-sources]].
